label: puzzle_content_rotation table
insight: New table stores rolling 30-day puzzle variants per class. active_from/active_until gate the current variant. Index on (puzzle_class, active_from, active_until) enables O(log n) lookup for getActivePuzzle.

label: completion_time_seconds computed column
insight: Generated ALWAYS AS column on member_puzzle_completions computes seconds from start/end timestamps at DB level — no application-layer subtraction needed. STORED persists the value to disk for index support.

label: spark_velocity_anomalies view
insight: Simple view over flagged completions — decouples the curator workflow from the raw table structure. Curator reads the view; application flags rows directly on the table.

label: checkRotationsDue cron helper
insight: Pure function returns puzzle classes expiring within 7 days — signal for content-ops to queue new variant. Does NOT generate content (human task). Returns empty array when all classes have healthy lookahead.

label: recordPuzzleCompletion: dual AND gate
insight: Flagging requires BOTH below-p5-percentile AND account-age < 30 days. Either condition alone is insufficient. This prevents penalizing experienced members who complete puzzles quickly and new members who complete slowly.

label: trust_match schema
insight: New schema isolates Trust Match state tables from the public schema. member_trust_state (one row per member) and member_defaults_log (immutable audit log) separate mutable state from immutable history.

label: getEffectiveSeasoningAge: floor at zero
insight: Effective age = current_age_days - 30 when penalty active, floored at 0. Member cannot have negative seasoning age even if very new. Penalty is temporary (30 days); full age restored afterward.

label: GSR trigger: 3 defaults in 90-day rolling window
insight: getDefaultsInWindow computes the rolling count; applySeasoningPenalty triggers GSR if new_count >= 3. Two defaults at days 1 and 80 do NOT trigger (count = 2 < 3). Three defaults anywhere in 90 days always trigger.

label: XP and Rep unaffected by penalty
insight: Design decision: Seasoning penalty affects ONLY effective Seasoning age. XP and Rep are untouched. This is deliberate — punishment is scoped to the trust-tiers subsystem, not the full reputation system.

label: quorum floor formula: mean × 0.70
insight: Floor = trailing 90-day mean rep-weighted votes × 0.70. The 0.70 multiplier gives a 30% slack so normal participation variations don't fail proposals; only coordinated quiet-window attacks fail.

label: low-visibility window detection
insight: Low-visibility = another proposal in the preceding 7 days attracted > 1.5× mean votes. Key insight: the floor only bites when BOTH conditions hold (low votes AND quiet window). Normal proposals always pass.

label: baseline_provisional flag
insight: When < 90 days of vote history exists, baseline is flagged provisional. Floor is still enforced but curator is informed that the floor may be imprecise. Prevents blocking legitimate governance during platform bootstrap.

label: 0.5% random sample with AND gate
insight: Three gates: amount >= 100 Marks, neither party < 30 days old, no prior Trust Match bond. Then 0.5% dice roll. Test confirms the rate holds over 10,000 trials (within 0.2%–0.8% tolerance).

label: inflated verdict: staged, not auto-applied
insight: submitAuditVerdict for 'inflated' sets consequenceQueued to a staging string but does NOT call reverseMarkTransaction or applySeasoningPenalty. Curator must confirm before any reversal. This is the most important guardrail in Phase D.

label: MarkQualityAuditPanel UI scaffold
insight: React component lists pending audits with transaction detail, member XP context, verdict buttons, and notes field. Warnings rendered inline. No direct DB writes — all actions go through onSubmitVerdict prop which the parent wires to the staged workflow.

label: cycle detector: bidirectional DFS with canonical deduplication
insight: Trust Match bonds are undirected. DFS finds A→B→C→A and A→C→B→A as separate paths. Canonical form: rotate to smallest member first, then pick lexicographically smaller of forward/reverse direction. This collapses both traversals to one key.

label: cycle length cap: 3-5 only
insight: Architectural constraint: cycles >= 6 are not flagged. Longer rings are statistically less likely to be coordinated. DB constraint CHECK (cycle_length BETWEEN 3 AND 5) enforces this at persistence layer.

label: runDailyCycleAudit: new vs existing logic
insight: Existing cycles (any verdict, including legitimate_collaboration) are matched by canonical key and get last_seen updated, not re-inserted. New cycles are inserted with verdict=pending. This prevents re-flagging approved legitimate collaborations.

label: TrustMatchCycleReview curator UI
insight: Three-button verdict UI: legitimate_collaboration / under_investigation / coordinated_ring. coordinated_ring consequences explicitly staged — UI text warns 'Founder confirmation required before applying.'

label: No auto-punitive actions anywhere in K501
insight: Critical design discipline: every Phase (A-E) flags for human review only. No Phase auto-denies, auto-reverses, or auto-penalizes. All consequential actions require curator review + (for coordinated_ring and mark reversal) Founder confirmation. Per B124 explicit guardrail.
